description: Model Hadamard matrix construction as a constraint satisfaction problem encoded in a tensor network. Each entry is a binary tensor variable. Orthogonality constraints become tensor contractions. Approximate contraction via BP or DMRG-like sweeps finds solutions.
mathematical_formalization: Construct tensor T_{r,s,k} = delta(sum_k h_{rk}*h_{sk}, 0) for r != s. Full constraint: T = prod_{r<s} T_{r,s}. Approximate via MPS with bond dimension chi.
analogical_connections: TN contraction ~ DMRG ground state finding
implementation_hypothesis: Represent each row as a tensor. DMRG-like sweep optimizing one row at a time. Adaptive bond dimension starting at chi=2.
experiment_seed: Toy version for order 28 using ITensor. Measure convergence and scaling to 44, 92.
